questions.title: Where does selected markup state live?
questions.context: viewerStore already holds activeTool (transient, not persisted). markupStore holds all markup data. A selectedMarkupId could go in either store, or a new thin selectionStore. This affects which components need to subscribe and how selection interacts with undo/redo.

options.label: viewerStore (transient, alongside activeTool)
options.description: Cleanest: selection is UI state like zoom/pan. Already imported everywhere markup tools are wired. No new store.

options.label: markupStore (alongside markup data)
options.description: Selection stays co-located with markup data. Simpler if delete-via-selection needs to interact with the undo stack directly.

options.label: New selectionStore (dedicated)
options.description: Maximum isolation but adds import surface. Overkill unless multi-select state becomes complex.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Selection visual — how does 'selected' look vs. 'hovered'?
questions.context: HoverRing (outer offset 4/zoom, ~40% opacity white) and PulseHighlight (outer offset 8/zoom) already exist in Layer 2. A 'selected' state needs to be visually distinct from hover. Options include a thicker/brighter ring, a dashed outline, or reusing HoverRing at 100% opacity.

options.label: Solid bright ring (same shape as HoverRing, 100% opacity, accent color)
options.description: Simple — reuse HoverRing component with opacity=1 and color=#0078d4 (accent). Hover stays white/40%.

options.label: Dashed/animated selection outline (distinct style)
options.description: More CAD-like ("marching ants"). Requires Konva dash animation (Konva.Animation on the dash offset). More implementation work.

options.label: Corner handles (transformer-style resize handles)
options.description: Shows a bounding-box with corner squares. Users could drag to re-position. Significant added scope — worth its own phase.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Click behavior when a markup tool is active
questions.context: Currently, clicking on the canvas with Count/Linear/Area/Perimeter/Wall active places a markup point. If a markup tool is active and the user clicks on an existing markup, should it select the markup (interrupting placement) or be ignored (placement takes priority)?

options.label: Placement always takes priority — selection only works in 'select' mode
options.description: Simplest. User must press Escape to cancel in-progress markup, then click to select. No ambiguity about intent.

options.label: Click on existing markup always selects it, even mid-placement
options.description: More discoverable. Requires detecting if click hit an existing markup before deciding to add a point. Complex hit-test logic.

options.label: Selection works in 'select' mode only; Escape from any tool returns to 'select' mode
options.description: Middle ground: Escape exits markup tools and leaves the user in select mode rather than returning to 'select' (which is the current idle). Feels more natural for mouse-first users.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Delete key — how does it interact with the existing right-click Delete?
questions.context: Right-click on a markup already shows a context menu with a Delete item (calls markupStore.deleteMarkup). Phase 9 adds Delete-key deletion when a markup is selected. Both paths should produce the same undoable MarkupCommand. Should they use the same code path?

options.label: Both use the same deleteMarkup store action — key handler just calls it
options.description: Single implementation. Delete key reads selectedMarkupId from store, calls deleteMarkup(id). Context menu does the same. DRY.

options.label: Delete key handler is separate, calls a new 'deleteSelected' action
options.description: Slightly cleaner separation for multi-select later (deleteSelected handles an array). More future-proof but adds surface now.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Clicking canvas empty space while in 'select' mode — what happens?
questions.context: When no markup is under the click, in standard selection UIs this deselects the current selection. But it could also be a no-op to avoid accidental deselects while navigating.

options.label: Clicking empty space deselects (clears selectedMarkupId)
options.description: Standard selection behavior. Clean, predictable.

options.label: Clicking empty space starts rubber-band drag (multi-select path)
options.description: Avoids a separate mode toggle for rubber-band — if there's nothing to select under cursor, drag draws the rectangle. More efficient but the interaction model is less explicit.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: How does rubber-band drag distinguish from canvas pan?
questions.context: Currently, middle-mouse-button drag pans the canvas (DOM event listeners). Left-mouse-button drag on the Stage currently does nothing in 'select' mode. Options include: rubber-band on any LMB drag in select mode, or require a modifier key (Alt/Shift) to activate rubber-band.

options.label: LMB drag on empty canvas in 'select' mode = rubber-band (no modifier)
options.description: Simplest UX. Middle-mouse still pans. Users in select mode expect LMB drag to rubber-band. Most consistent with CAD tools.

options.label: Shift+LMB drag = rubber-band; LMB drag alone = pan (even in select mode)
options.description: Panning always available with LMB even in select mode. Matches browser-based PDF viewers. Requires users to know the modifier.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Containment rule — full inside vs. touch/intersect?
questions.context: When the rubber-band rectangle is released, which markups are selected? Full containment (markup bounding box entirely within the rubber band) is predictable but misses markups that straddle the edge. Touch/intersect (any overlap) is more forgiving for large area/perimeter polygons.

options.label: Markup centroid/anchor point inside rubber band
options.description: Simplest computation. Count pins use their anchor point; polylines/polygons use their centroid. Unambiguous for all markup types.

options.label: Any markup point inside rubber band (touch/intersect)
options.description: More forgiving for large polygons. A wall or area polygon that barely overlaps is still caught. More computation (walk all points).

options.label: Full bounding box of markup entirely inside rubber band
options.description: Standard CAD 'crossing window vs. selection window' behavior. Most predictable for estimators familiar with CAD.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Ctrl+A — select all markups on current page?
questions.context: isTextInputActive() guard is already in place for global keyboard shortcuts. Ctrl+A is unused. Adding it is low-effort. But it's a minor nice-to-have and could be left for a later phase.

options.label: Yes — add Ctrl+A to select all markups on current page
options.description: Pairs naturally with the rubber-band and Delete flow. Minimal extra code.

options.label: No — skip Ctrl+A for now, in scope of this phase only if trivial
options.description: Keep scope tight. Rubber-band + Delete is the primary feature. Ctrl+A can come later.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Group delete undo — how is 'remove multiple markups' stored in MarkupCommand history?
questions.context: MarkupCommand currently supports 'add' | 'delete' | 'edit' | 'recolor' for a single markup. Group delete of N markups needs to be one undoable action. Options: a new 'delete-group' command that holds an array, or push N individual 'delete' commands and undo them as a batch.

options.label: New 'delete-group' MarkupCommand variant holding Markup[]
options.description: Single undo step for the group. Clean semantics. Requires extending the MarkupCommand discriminated union and the undo/redo switch in markupStore.

options.label: Push N individual 'delete' commands; undo pops N times automatically
options.description: No new command type. But Ctrl+Z would undo one markup at a time, not the whole group — inconsistent with user expectation of one-step undo.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Which modals get the draggable+centered treatment?
questions.context: The phase goal says 'every modal that appears in the app'. Current modals: CalibrationDialog (Set Scale), MarkupNamePopup (name/category/color input), ScalePopup (scale confirm), SaveCloseModal, OpenErrorModal, UncalibratedExportWarningModal, ArchiveCorruptedModal, DimensionMismatchModal, PageCountAbortModal. MarkupContextMenu is a context menu, not a modal — likely excluded.

options.label: All true modals (everything with an overlay/backdrop) — 8-9 components
options.description: Complete scope. Covers CalibrationDialog, MarkupNamePopup, ScalePopup, SaveCloseModal, OpenErrorModal, UncalibratedExportWarningModal, ArchiveCorruptedModal, DimensionMismatchModal, PageCountAbortModal.

options.label: Only the 'workflow' modals — CalibrationDialog, MarkupNamePopup, ScalePopup
options.description: Focus on the modals the estimator interacts with repeatedly during takeoff. Error/confirmation modals (SaveClose, OpenError, etc.) keep their current centered-non-draggable behavior.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Draggable implementation — shared wrapper or per-modal?
questions.context: Modals are currently individual components (CalibrationDialog, MarkupNamePopup, etc.) with inline overlay + inner div. A reusable DraggableModal wrapper component could handle centering + drag logic once, then each modal just renders its content inside it.

options.label: Shared DraggableModal wrapper component
options.description: Write drag logic once. Each modal renders children inside the wrapper. Clean, DRY. Requires refactoring each modal to remove its own overlay div.

options.label: Add useDraggable hook, each modal applies it independently
options.description: Keeps per-modal structure intact. Hook returns position offset + onPointerDown handler for the drag handle. More flexible but slightly more repetition.

options.label: Per-modal, inline drag state (no shared abstraction)
options.description: Easiest to diff per modal. But duplicates drag logic (mousedown, mousemove, mouseup) N times. Hard to keep consistent.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Drag handle — where on the modal does dragging work?
questions.context:
MarkupNamePopup has no explicit title bar — it starts directly with inputs. CalibrationDialog has a

Set Scale

heading. Dragging could work on a dedicated header strip, or on the entire modal (anywhere not occupied by an interactive control).

options.label: Dedicated header bar on every modal (add one to modals that lack it)
options.description: Clear affordance — cursor changes to 'grab' on the header strip. Every modal gets a consistent title bar. Adds a small UI strip (~28px) to modals that currently lack one.

options.label: Drag on the modal's existing title/heading element only
options.description: No layout change. For CalibrationDialog it's the <h2>; for MarkupNamePopup the top strip could become draggable. Might be too small a hit area.

options.label: Drag anywhere on the modal that isn't an input/button
options.description: Maximum hit area. But no visual affordance — user might not know the modal is draggable.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Backdrop click — keep or remove for currently-dismissible modals?
questions.context: CalibrationDialog currently closes on backdrop click (e.target === e.currentTarget). With a draggable overlay, pointerdown on the backdrop could start a drag of a modal near the edge. Should backdrop-dismiss stay, or should all modals require explicit button dismissal?

options.label: Keep backdrop-dismiss for applicable modals (CalibrationDialog, ScalePopup, MarkupNamePopup)
options.description: Familiar behavior. Risk of accidental dismiss during drag is low if drag is confined to the modal header. Monitor in UAT.

options.label: Remove backdrop-dismiss — all modals require an explicit Cancel/Close button
options.description: Safer with draggable modals. No accidental dismiss. Consistent across all modals. Slightly more clicks to dismiss.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Modal position memory — reset on close or persist across opens?
questions.context: The phase says 'position maintained until the modal is closed'. After closing and re-opening, should the modal re-center, or remember its last position (e.g. via localStorage or module-level ref)?

options.label: Re-center on every open (position resets when modal closes)
options.description: Simplest. No persistence logic. Always predictable starting position. The user repositions each time they open if desired.

options.label: Remember last position per modal type (module-level ref, not localStorage)
options.description: Quality of life — CalibrationDialog stays where the user left it from the last calibration. Module-level ref is simple and ephemeral (resets on app restart).

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Replace vs. wrap — what happens to the current flat action bar?
questions.context: The current Toolbar.tsx is a 40px flex bar with Open, Save, SaveAs, Replace, Export (left), Page nav (center), Set Scale (center-right), 5 markup tools, Zoom controls (right). The phase says 'replaces (or wraps)'. If replaced, the existing component gets deleted and rebuilt as a ribbon. If wrapped, the ribbon renders above or alongside the old bar.

options.label: Full replacement — delete Toolbar.tsx and build ribbon from scratch
options.description: Clean break. All buttons migrated into ribbon tabs. No legacy layout code. More work upfront, cleaner result.

options.label: Wrap — add ribbon tabs above, keep existing toolbar below as the panel
options.description: Faster to implement. The existing Toolbar.tsx becomes the 'default tab panel'. Tab row sits above it. Risk: awkward double-height bar.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Ribbon button style — icon-above-label (tall square) vs. icon+label inline?
questions.context: The current toolbar uses icon+label inline, 28px height (small, horizontal). A ribbon typically uses icon-above-label, taller square buttons (~56-64px). The phase says 'square icon+label buttons'. This would require a new button component styled differently from the existing IconButton.

options.label: Icon above label — tall square buttons (~56-64px), ribbon style
options.description: Matches Microsoft Office ribbon conventions. More visual; slower to scan. Requires new button component distinct from existing IconButton.

options.label: Icon+label inline — same as current IconButton, just in ribbon tab panels
options.description: Reuses existing IconButton component. Ribbon feels like grouped sections of the current bar. Less visual change but less distinct from the old toolbar.

options.label: Mixed — large icon-above-label for primary actions, small inline for secondary
options.description: Matches modern ribbon conventions (Word/Excel: big Paste, small Cut/Copy). More nuanced layout, more design decisions per tab.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Home tab — what goes here?
questions.context: Home is typically the default/first tab with most-used actions. Current most-used: Open, Save, SaveAs, Replace Plan PDF, Export. Could also include Undo/Redo here.

options.label: Open, Save, SaveAs, Replace PDF, Export (current left-side buttons)
options.description: Mirrors current toolbar left section. Predictable mapping.

options.label: Open, Save, SaveAs, Replace PDF, Export + Undo/Redo
options.description: Undo/Redo move from keyboard-only to ribbon-visible. Good for discoverability.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Tools tab — what goes here?
questions.context: The 5 markup tools (Count, Linear, Area, Perimeter, Wall) plus Set Scale logically belong in a Tools tab. The selection tool (new in this phase) would also live here.

options.label: Select (new), Count, Linear, Area, Perimeter, Wall, Set Scale
options.description: All markup + scale tools in one place. Mirrors current toolbar markup section.

options.label: Markup tools here; Scale tools in Page tab
options.description: Scale calibration is page-specific — fits Page tab. Tools tab is purely markup placement.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: View tab — what goes here?
questions.context: View controls: Zoom In, Zoom Out, Fit to Window, zoom % display. Could also include TotalsPanel show/hide toggle and per-markup visibility (show/hide all hidden items). Currently these are distributed across toolbar (zoom) and totals panel (lightbulb icons).

options.label: Zoom In, Zoom Out, Fit to Window — just the current zoom controls
options.description: Minimal, clean. Panel show/hide stays in the panel itself.

options.label: Zoom controls + Show/Hide Totals Panel + Show All / Hide All markups
options.description: Consolidates all visibility controls in View. More discoverable than hunting for the panel toggle.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Settings and Help tabs — what do they show?
questions.context: These tabs are named in the phase goal but their content isn't specified. Settings could be a placeholder or open an Electron preferences window. Help could show keyboard shortcuts or link to documentation.

options.label: Both stubbed — tab labels only, panel shows 'Coming soon'
options.description: Fastest. Structure is correct, content deferred. No risk of scope creep.

options.label: Help shows keyboard shortcuts list; Settings stubbed
options.description: Help provides real value (shortcuts reference). Settings stubbed for later. Good trade-off.

options.label: Settings shows global unit preference; Help shows keyboard shortcuts
options.description: Settings gets a meaningful first feature: switch between m/ft/mm for display. This is a real gap — currently unit is set per-calibration only.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Default active tab on app load
questions.context: Which ribbon tab should be active when the app starts? The estimator's first action is usually opening a file, so Home makes sense. But Tools is where they spend most time.

options.label: Home tab (default — Open/Save actions most prominent on start)
options.description: First action is always opening a file. Home is logical.

options.label: Tools tab (markup tools most used during takeoff)
options.description: Once a file is open, most time is spent in markup tools. Default to what the user does most.

options.label: Remember last-used tab (localStorage)
options.description: Quality of life. User who always works in Tools doesn't have to switch every session.

options.label: Custom

sections.title: Enter / Double-click to Finish In-Progress Markup

questions.title: Double-click — anywhere on canvas, or only near the last placed point?
questions.context: For Linear/Area/Perimeter/Wall, each click adds a segment point. Double-clicking to commit could work (A) anywhere on the canvas (any double-click = commit), or (B) only within a hit radius of the last placed point (similar to start-vertex close detection). The ROADMAP says 'double-clicking the most recently placed point'.

options.label: Anywhere on canvas — any double-click commits the in-progress markup
options.description: Simplest implementation. The second click of the double-click also places a point (before commit fires), so the markup gains one extra point — acceptable if the gap is <5px.

options.label: Within hit radius of last placed point only (e.g. 12px)
options.description: Matches ROADMAP wording. More deliberate — user must double-click the last point. Prevents accidental commits. Requires the same hit-test pattern as isOverStartPoint.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Enter key — what is the minimum-point guard?
questions.context: Should Enter commit the in-progress markup regardless of how many points are placed, or be blocked for degenerate shapes (e.g. < 2 points for linear = no line, < 3 points for polygon = no area)?

options.label: Block commit if markup would be degenerate (< 2 points for linear, < 3 for polygon)
options.description: Prevents meaningless markups. Consistent with existing close-polygon detection (needs 3+ points). Silent ignore or visual feedback.

options.label: Always commit — even if degenerate; let the store handle edge cases
options.description: Simpler key handler. But a 1-point 'linear' markup is a bug waiting to happen (zero length, no label).

options.label: Custom
